channel.title: New Word Wall
channel.description: Recent content on New Word Wall

item.title: Doomscrolling
item.description:
Definition

Compulsively scrolling through negative or anxiety-inducing news or social media content.

Origin

The term emerged during the COVID-19 pandemic and became associated with excessive digital media consumption.

Example

“I stayed up until 2 AM doomscrolling TikTok and news feeds.”

Related Terms

  • digital fatigue
  • algorithmic anxiety
  • information overload

Singaid (idiom, idiomatic expression)

Pronunciation

/siŋ-eɪd/ or /sɪŋ-eɪd/

sing as in “sing” or “sin” with a g (depending on dialect) aid as in “aid”—offering help.

Defintion

Said to someone who has just hiccuped, as a polite remark; a remark offering good wishes of hiccups passing.

Like the English expression bless you, which is often used when someone is sneezing, singaid is a polite expression towards someone with the hiccups.

Use of the word singaid

person one: *has the hiccups*
